@graph.name: How to Improve Your Brain Longevity with CogniFit
@graph.description: A 4-step personalized brain longevity plan to measure, train, and protect your cognitive health as you age.

step.name: Assess Your Brain
step.text: Take a scientifically validated cognitive assessment to measure 20+ cognitive skills and establish your baseline.

step.name: Get a Personalized Program
step.text: CogniFit's patented ITS technology creates a training program adapted to your unique cognitive profile.

step.name: Train What Matters
step.text: Complete short, engaging 15-20 minute sessions 3-4 times per week targeting your weakest cognitive areas.

step.name: Track Your Cognitive Age
step.text: Monitor your progress with clear reports showing how your brain evolves over time and stay motivated with milestones.

mainEntity.name: What is brain longevity, and why should I care?

acceptedAnswer.text: Brain longevity is your ability to preserve memory, focus, reasoning, and decision-making as you age. Unlike a sore knee, cognitive decline sneaks up on you. Most people don't notice until it's already affecting their daily life. Research shows you can actively build neural reserves through targeted training. CogniFit's brain longevity test measures 20+ cognitive skills so you know exactly where you stand, and over 6 million people are already using it to protect their independence.

mainEntity.name: Does brain training actually work, or is it just hype?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, when it's done right. The landmark ACTIVE study, published in the Journal of the American Geriatrics Society, tracked thousands of older adults and found that cognitive training benefits lasted up to 10 years. CogniFit's programs have been used in over 1,083 clinical trials because they challenge the specific areas where your brain needs it most.

mainEntity.name: Which mental abilities start fading first?

acceptedAnswer.text: Processing speed starts slowing down in your late 20s. In your 40s and 50s, working memory and executive function begin to dip. By your 60s, recalling specific events gets harder. However, vocabulary, wisdom, and accumulated knowledge often keep getting better with age. CogniFit measures all 20+ of these skills individually.

mainEntity.name: What makes CogniFit different from other brain training apps?

acceptedAnswer.text: CogniFit was built by neuroscientists in 1999, used in 1,083+ clinical trials, trusted by 3,530+ healthcare professionals, and adopted by 6,438+ research institutions. It uses patented ITS technology that adapts in real-time to your performance. You get a real Cognitive Age score, not just a game score.

mainEntity.name: How quickly will I actually notice a difference?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most people start feeling sharper within 2-4 weeks, with more focused conversations, faster recall, and clearer thinking. Just 15-20 minutes, 3-4 times a week. Clinical studies show measurable improvements after about 8 weeks, and the ACTIVE trial found benefits can last 5-10 years.

mainEntity.name: Is there real science behind CogniFit?

acceptedAnswer.text: CogniFit is one of the most researched cognitive platforms in the world, with over 1,083 clinical trials at institutions like Harvard Medical School, University of Washington, and Tel Aviv University. Assessments have been validated against gold-standard neuropsychological tests. Over 3,530 healthcare professionals use CogniFit for clinical assessment and cognitive rehabilitation.
